capacityPlan.reasonForWaveASplit: Two makers × nine odds pairs would require 18 anonymous streams, above the live cap of 13. wave-a-ms uses 12 anonymous + 2 owner streams; wave-a-total uses 6 anonymous + 2 owner streams.

agentFrontendIntegration.caveat: same configured provider route; no real frontend registry adapter claim
agentFrontendIntegration.finding: four distinct model IDs and signer mappings exercised

docsFootguns.result: current module addresses restored and every stale-module allowance revoked to zero

harnessOperator.finding: live API SSE cap made an all-nine-pair concurrent two-maker wave unsafe
harnessOperator.result: capacity-safe, target-scoped subruns preserved same healthy targets and later games

frozenPick.rationale: Atlanta combines the wave's best record with the strongest listed pitching matchup, and plus money supports taking the favorite on the run line.

Atlanta enters with the stronger 54-38 record versus St. Louis at 48-44, and the listed starter matchup favors Atlanta. The available lineup and pre-game status support a straightforward moneyline selection.
Minnesota has the stronger season record and home-field advantage against Los Angeles, supporting the moneyline selection.
Atlanta enters with the stronger record in the frozen bundle, posted lineup confirmation, and a clear reference-odds favorite profile behind Chris Sale in a game with only light wind noted.
